title: Pattern Geometry IR v0
description: Semantic intermediate representation for 2D pattern systems. Describes what a pattern means — motifs, arrangement, repetition, symmetry, rendering intent — independent of any specific geometry backend. Backends (Maker.js, SVG, DXF, etc.) receive this IR and produce low-level geometry.

version.description: Schema version identifier. Must be exactly 'pg-ir-v0' for this revision.

pattern.description: Pattern identity: what this pattern is called and how it is classified.

id.description: Unique machine-readable identifier for this pattern instance.

description.description: Short prose description of the pattern's character and intent.

classification.description: Structural classification of the pattern arrangement.

geometry.description: Semantic geometry: what the pattern is made of — not how to draw it. This is the 'recipe' layer above backends.

tiling.description: The tiling/arrangement system that governs motif placement.

type.description: Category of tiling. 'none' used for freeform arrangements.

motifs.description: Motif definitions: the semantic building blocks placed on tiles. Each entry describes the figure family applied to a tile type, plus optional parameters. This is the core semantic payload — backends interpret these to produce actual geometry.

tileType.description: The tile shape this motif applies to (e.g. 'hexagon', 'octagon', 'square').

family.description: Figure family: the semantic category of the motif.

count.description: Number of tiles carrying this motif.

parameters.description: Family-specific parameters (e.g. skip, delta for stars; depth, scale for rosettes). Schema is intentionally open here — specific parameters are validated by the target backend, not the IR schema.

symmetry.description: Symmetry metadata. Optional: backends may compute this, or it may be declared.

wallpaperGroup.description: Wallpaper/crystallographic group (e.g. 'p6m', 'p4m', 'p1'), or null if not applicable.

rotational.description: Maximum rotational symmetry order, or null if not applicable.

reflectional.description: Whether the pattern has reflection symmetry.

style.description: Rendering intent: how the pattern should be visually presented. Style is semantic guidance for backends, not pixel-exact CSS.

renderMode.description: Primary rendering mode. 'line' = stroke-only, 'fill' = filled shapes, 'interlace' = over/under weave, 'band' = ribbon band.

metadata.description: Optional metadata extensions. Not required for pattern generation — these are discovery, provenance, and licensing fields. The core IR must validate without any metadata.
